task_name: Define Builder Task Create Readiness Contract
goal: Define the document-only Builder Task Create Readiness Contract after Auth/Persistence Readiness without creating Builder tasks, executing Builder calls, or enabling auth, persistence, or approval records.
claims: BP-067 defines only a document-level Builder Task Create Readiness Contract after Auth/Persistence Readiness.
BP-067 keeps Builder Task Create, live Builder calls, execution, auth, secrets, DB, persistence, and approval recording blocked.
BP-067 does not implement UI, auth, secrets, DB, persistence, approval action, task creation, or Builder integration.
assumptions: Auth/Persistence Readiness Candidate output is the required source for later Builder Task Create readiness input.
Task Create readiness can be assessed locally before any Builder call exists.
Actual Builder Task Create needs later explicit live-adapter, auth, persistence, approval, and evidence decisions.
dod: The document defines required input, output, gates, blocked behavior, and non-goals.
The document explicitly prevents Builder Task Create, live Builder calls, Execute, Approve, Push, Deploy, auth, secrets, DB, persistence, and approval recording.
stop_conditions: Runtime, fixture, package, or tool changes become necessary
Builder Task Create, live Builder, auth, secrets, persistence, approval recording, DB, deploy, execution, approve, or push becomes necessary
prior_task_findings: BP-066 identified Builder Task Create Readiness Contract as the next safe boundary after the local Auth/Persistence Readiness chain.
